summary: High‑energy Pocky Way > Playing > Rider > Terrapin with tight jams
blurb: The second set erupts with a blazing Hey Pocky Way → Playing In The Band → I Know You Rider → Terrapin Station sequence and a monster Terrapin jam. Jerry’s tone is flawless, Brent’s keyboards drive the heat, and the rhythm section locks in throughout. Standout moments include Stagger Lee, Memphis Blues, and Throwing Stones.
review: On August 5 1989 at Cal Expo the Dead delivered one of their quintessential late‑summer shows. The first set warmed up with solid rockers—Cold Rain And Snow, We Can Run, and a ripping Stagger Lee solo—but it was the second set that truly ignited the crowd. A scorching Hey Pocky Way segued into Playing In The Band, then into I Know You Rider before exploding into an expansive Terrapin Station that featured a four‑minute drum break and relentless energy from both drummers. Brent Mydland’s keyboards were on fire, pushing the jam forward while Jerry Garcia’s guitar tone was “perfect” and his solo work, especially on Stagger Lee, drew unanimous praise. Phil Lesh kept a solid foundation despite a few minor slip notes, and the band’s chemistry felt tight from start to finish, making this a must‑listen for fans of the ’89 era.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five of seven recordings are distinctly positive, repeatedly praising the high‑energy Pocky Way > Playing > Rider > Terrapin sequence, tight set flow, and stellar individual performances; no consistent negative comments appear, just two mixed notes about recording artifacts. This strong consensus warrants a solid +1.6 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Crisp, clear soundboard capture with excellent mix and no drop‑outs; showcases the full set and highlights the iconic Pocky Way → Playing → Rider → Terrapin sequence.

key_highlights: Blazing Hey Pocky Way → Playing In The Band → I Know You Rider → Terrapin Station medley
Monster Terrapin Station jam with a four‑minute drum break
Stagger Lee solo and Memphis Blues (Stuck Inside of Mobile with the Memphis Blues Again)
Tight, high‑energy second set flow and seamless transitions
song_highlights: Stuck Inside of Mobile with the Memphis Blues Again

band_performance.Jerry: Tone was perfect and "on fire" all night; delivered a beautiful solo on Stagger Lee and stellar guitar work throughout the set.
band_performance.Phil: Solid bass foundation with occasional slip notes; highlighted for his responsive interplay during the Pocky Way sequence and subtle 9±n note reply in the second set.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboards were on fire, driving the high‑energy sound; provided warm organ work on Row Jimmy and sustained presence throughout the jams.
band_performance.Billy: Drums shone with a four‑minute break before Terrapin Station; overall rhythm section praised for its energy and tightness.

must_listen_sequences: Stuck Inside of Mobile with the Memphis Blues Again
